executive_summary: Global systems exhibit moderate volatility characterised by a sharp energy fluctuation in the Middle East and persistent supply chain friction. Despite elevated media sensationalism surrounding Taiwan physical indicators remain highly stable and global fuel reserves are currently secure. The ongoing expansion of the artificial intelligence bubble alongside widening wealth disparity presents a latent threat to macroeconomic stability.
correlations: The volatility in Middle East energy markets directly compounds the systemic vulnerability of global supply chains which are already operating under elevated operational strain.
